violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to apply for a RICFSM with RIDOH. Application was printed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Molluscan Shellfish tags are not maintained for 90 days. Molluscan shellfish tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The doors to the outside, located in the kitchen and dining, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Several rat burrows observed behind dumpster area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Dumpster area must be maintained free of clutter, litter, and debris to prevent pest harborage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The 1 door freezer gasket was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. -Walk-in cooler shelves -Ice machine -Dry ingredient bins Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026
The 3-bay sink plumbing was observed leaking and in poor repair. Establishment is to fix leak. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The coving at the bar area is in poor repair. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Coving must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The floor behind the cook line and service counter area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per interview with PIC, chef is in process of completing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, whisk) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by small dead insects in traps at the bar area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of flour. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
At time of inspection, tequila bottles are being reused for juice at the bar. SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES may not be reused. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
Refrigerator and prep unit door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

Refrigerator located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, ambient temperature was observed at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. No TCS foods in unit at time of inspection. Per interview with PIC, part has been ordered. PIC informed that no storage of TCS foods until unit reaches an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The pre-flush spray hose was not operable. Inspector observed hot and cold handles missing, unable to turn on at time of inspection. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Per interview with PIC, part has been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The wall junctures in the mop sink area do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The following areas have an accumulation of dust and other debris. 1. Hose/piping (ware washing area) 2. Light (ware washing area) 3. Walls/ceiling (Ware washing area) The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the walk-in cooler. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
